title: pkgconfig `Version` field confuses some processing tools
body:
At least on the version in Ubuntu 18.04, the contents of the pkgconfig file is the following:

```
prefix=/usr
exec_prefix=${prefix}
datarootdir=${prefix}/share
includedir=${datarootdir}/verilator/include

Name: verilator
Description: fast free Verilog simulator
URL: http://www.veripool.org/verilator
Version: 3.916 2017-11-25
Requires:
Libs:
Cflags: -I${includedir} -I${includedir}/vltstd
```
Note the value of `Version` at `3.916 2017-11-25`, i.e. a dot-separated version number, followed by a space, then a dash-separated date.

Now, I haven't looked up the pkgconfig spec, maybe this is a perfectly cromulent version value; but in pragmatic terms, it has the potential to confuse other tools. My example is the Haskell packaging tool Cabal, which has support for pkgconfig dependencies, but it barfs on the above value with:

```
setup: parsing output of pkg-config --modversion failed
```
code_blocks: setup: parsing output of pkg-config --modversion failed
